intent: Produce a Timeline / Evolution report tracing the 26-year history of renewable energy costs from 2000 to 2026. The report is NOT a buying guide; it is a chronological narrative + current-day cost analysis. Ground in >= 120 sandbox URLs (>= 60 cited). (A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 30 renewable energy system products with cost data + performance metrics. (B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from /f/technology, /f/energy, /f/Environment, /f/Science. (C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles, mandatory: Renewable energy, Solar energy, Wind power, Battery energy storage, Energy cost, Electric power transmission, Energy policy, Energy market, Energy conservation, Energy infrastructure. Output a chronological timeline divided into 3 eras (Era 1: 2000-2005, Era 2: 2006-2015, Era 3: 2016-2026). For each era: >= 3 wiki articles defining the cost trends, >= 3 shopping products with cost data, >= 3 reddit threads discussing cost changes. Add a modern cost comparison of renewable energy technologies — a markdown nested list with each leaf citing a shopping product + a wiki article. End with '7 myths the community now considers debunked' — each myth cited with a wiki + reddit pair. Format: markdown links only. Begin with Era 1. No chain-of-thought.
author_notes: Deep-tier task generated by gen_task_spec.py from configs/deep_topics/renewable_energy_cost_timeline.yaml. Golden: must=143, pool=726.
